domain_true.statement: Greta Thunberg began her school strike for climate outside the Swedish parliament in August 2018.
The Fridays for Future movement was inspired by Greta Thunberg's solo school strike.
Greta Thunberg addressed the United Nations Climate Action Summit in September 2019.
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels.
Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas that traps heat in Earth's atmosphere.
The burning of fossil fuels is the largest source of human-caused carbon dioxide emissions.
Methane is a more potent greenhouse gas than carbon dioxide over a 20-year period.
The IPCC was established in 1988 by the World Meteorological Organization and the United Nations Environment Programme.
The IPCC publishes assessment reports synthesizing climate science from thousands of researchers.
Arctic sea ice extent has declined significantly since satellite records began in 1979.
Global average surface temperature has risen by more than 1 degree Celsius since the pre-industrial era.
The ocean absorbs a large portion of the excess heat trapped by greenhouse gases.
Ocean acidification is caused by the absorption of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
Coral reefs are highly vulnerable to warming ocean temperatures and acidification.
Deforestation contributes to climate change by releasing stored carbon and reducing carbon absorption.
The Amazon rainforest serves as a major carbon sink for the planet.
Greta Thunberg sailed across the Atlantic Ocean in 2019 to attend climate summits without flying.
Greta Thunberg was named Time magazine's Person of the Year in 2019.
Renewable energy sources include solar, wind, hydroelectric, and geothermal power.
Solar photovoltaic energy converts sunlight directly into electricity.
Wind turbines generate electricity by converting kinetic energy from wind into mechanical power.
The cost of solar energy has declined dramatically over the past decade.
The cost of onshore wind energy has decreased significantly since 2010.
Fossil fuels include coal, oil, and natural gas.
Coal is the most carbon-intensive fossil fuel per unit of energy produced.
The energy sector is the largest source of global greenhouse gas emissions.
Permafrost contains large amounts of stored carbon that can be released as it thaws.
Sea levels are rising due to thermal expansion of water and melting of land-based ice.
Glaciers around the world have been losing mass at an accelerating rate.
The Greenland ice sheet is losing mass and contributing to sea level rise.
The Antarctic ice sheet is also losing mass and contributing to sea level rise.
The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report was released in stages between 2021 and 2023.
Nitrous oxide is a greenhouse gas with a global warming potential much higher than carbon dioxide.
Transportation is a major source of greenhouse gas emissions globally.
Electric vehicles produce zero direct tailpipe emissions during operation.
Greta Thunberg has Asperger syndrome, which she has publicly discussed as influencing her activism.
Greta Thunberg published the book "No One Is Too Small to Make a Difference."
The Fridays for Future movement has organized global climate strikes involving millions of participants.
The Keeling Curve tracks the continuous rise of atmospheric carbon dioxide measured at Mauna Loa Observatory.
Atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration has exceeded 420 parts per million.
Pre-industrial atmospheric carbon dioxide levels were near 280 parts per million.
The greenhouse effect is a natural process that warms Earth's surface and makes it habitable.
Human activities have intensified the natural greenhouse effect, causing additional warming.
Climate change increases the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events such as heatwaves.
Droughts are projected to become more severe in many regions due to climate change.
Heavy precipitation events are becoming more frequent in a warming climate.
The Paris Agreement was adopted in December 2015 at COP21.
The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change organizes annual Conference of the Parties meetings.
Nationally Determined Contributions are climate action plans submitted by countries under the Paris Agreement.
Carbon neutrality means achieving a balance between carbon emissions produced and carbon removed from the atmosphere.
Net zero emissions means reducing greenhouse gas emissions as close to zero as possible with remaining emissions offset.
Greta Thunberg spoke at the World Economic Forum in Davos in January 2019.
Greta Thunberg told world leaders at the UN "How dare you" in her 2019 speech.
Sweden is Greta Thunberg's country of birth.
The IPCC does not conduct original research but assesses existing scientific literature.
The IPCC has produced six major assessment reports since its founding.
Climate feedback loops can amplify or dampen the effects of initial warming.
The albedo effect refers to the reflectivity of surfaces, with ice and snow reflecting more sunlight than darker surfaces.
Loss of ice cover reduces Earth's albedo, leading to further warming in a positive feedback loop.
Wetlands are important carbon sinks that store large amounts of organic carbon.
Peatlands store twice as much carbon as all the world's forests combined.
Mangrove forests sequester carbon at rates higher than most terrestrial forests per unit area.
Agriculture is a significant source of methane and nitrous oxide emissions.
Livestock farming produces methane through enteric fermentation in ruminant animals.
Rice paddies are a notable source of methane emissions.
The ozone layer and the greenhouse effect are distinct atmospheric phenomena.
Chlorofluorocarbons are both ozone-depleting substances and potent greenhouse gases.
The Montreal Protocol successfully reduced emissions of ozone-depleting substances.
Carbon capture and storage technology aims to capture carbon dioxide emissions at their source and store them underground.
Reforestation involves planting trees on land that was previously forested.
Afforestation involves planting trees on land that was not previously forested.
Greta Thunberg has been nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize multiple times.
The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change shared the Nobel Peace Prize in 2007 with Al Gore.
Fossil fuel subsidies remain substantial in many countries worldwide.
Divestment campaigns encourage institutions to withdraw investments from fossil fuel companies.
The European Union has established the European Green Deal as a climate policy framework.
China is the world's largest emitter of carbon dioxide from fossil fuel combustion.
The United States is the largest cumulative emitter of carbon dioxide in history.
Small island developing states are disproportionately vulnerable to sea level rise.
Climate change poses risks to global food security through impacts on crop yields.
Water scarcity is projected to worsen in many regions due to climate change.
Heat-related mortality is increasing as global temperatures rise.
Wildfires are becoming more frequent and severe in many regions due to hotter and drier conditions.
The cryosphere includes all frozen water on Earth, including glaciers, ice sheets, and permafrost.
Thermohaline circulation is a global ocean current system driven by differences in temperature and salinity.
Climate models are used to project future climate conditions under different emissions scenarios.
The IPCC uses Representative Concentration Pathways and Shared Socioeconomic Pathways to model future scenarios.
Greta Thunberg co-authored "The Climate Book," published in 2022.
Renewable energy capacity additions have outpaced fossil fuel capacity additions in recent years globally.
Battery storage technology is essential for managing the intermittency of solar and wind energy.
Hydroelectric power is the largest source of renewable electricity generation worldwide.
Geothermal energy harnesses heat from within the Earth to generate electricity or provide heating.
Offshore wind farms can generate more consistent energy than onshore installations due to stronger ocean winds.
Energy efficiency improvements reduce greenhouse gas emissions by lowering total energy demand.
Buildings account for a significant share of global energy consumption and related emissions.
The cement industry is a major industrial source of carbon dioxide emissions.
Steel production is a significant contributor to industrial greenhouse gas emissions.
Methane has a shorter atmospheric lifetime than carbon dioxide but a much higher warming potential per molecule.
The Global Methane Pledge launched at COP26 aims to reduce methane emissions by 30 percent by 2030.
COP26 was held in Glasgow, Scotland, in November 2021.
COP28 was held in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, in late 2023.
Greta Thunberg has criticized world leaders for insufficient action on climate change.
The carbon budget is the total amount of carbon dioxide that can be emitted while limiting warming to a specific target.
The remaining carbon budget for limiting warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius is rapidly shrinking.
Climate justice emphasizes that those least responsible for emissions often suffer the most from climate impacts.
Indigenous communities are disproportionately affected by the impacts of climate change.
Biodiversity loss is accelerated by climate change through habitat disruption and shifting ecosystems.
Ocean warming is causing marine species to shift their ranges toward the poles.
The IPCC has stated that human influence on the climate system is unequivocal.
Greenhouse gas emissions must reach net zero by mid-century to limit warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius.
Greta Thunberg's activism has inspired youth climate movements in countries across every continent.
Fossil fuel combustion releases not only carbon dioxide but also air pollutants harmful to human health.
Transitioning to renewable energy creates employment opportunities in new energy sectors.
Carbon pricing mechanisms include carbon taxes and cap-and-trade systems.
The European Union Emissions Trading System is the world's largest carbon market.
Climate adaptation involves adjusting systems and practices to reduce vulnerability to climate impacts.
Climate mitigation involves reducing or preventing greenhouse gas emissions.
Tipping points in the climate system are thresholds beyond which changes become self-reinforcing and irreversible.
The collapse of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet is considered a potential climate tipping point.
Greta Thunberg has consistently called for listening to climate scientists and following the science.

domain_false.statement: Greta Thunberg founded the Fridays for Future movement in 2016 at age thirteen.
The Paris Agreement was signed in 2018 by all United Nations member states.
The IPCC was established in 1992 by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change.
Greta Thunberg won the Nobel Peace Prize in 2019 for her climate activism.
The Kyoto Protocol was the first international treaty to set binding targets for renewable energy adoption.
Carbon dioxide accounts for roughly 90 percent of all human-caused greenhouse gas emissions.
The IPCC's first assessment report was published in 1995.
Greta Thunberg addressed the United Nations General Assembly for the first time in 2017.
Solar panels generate more electricity at night due to reduced atmospheric interference.
The Paris Agreement commits all signatory nations to achieving net-zero emissions by 2030.
Methane has a lower global warming potential than carbon dioxide over a 100-year period.
The IPCC directly conducts original scientific research on climate change.
Greta Thunberg sailed across the Atlantic Ocean in 2018 to attend a climate summit in Madrid.
Wind turbines are the largest single source of bird mortality worldwide.
The greenhouse effect was first described by Albert Einstein in 1905.
Global sea levels have risen by approximately three meters since the year 2000.
The IPCC's Sixth Assessment Report was released in 2018.
Greta Thunberg was born in Stockholm, Norway, in 2003.
Nuclear energy produces more lifecycle carbon emissions than natural gas power plants.
The ozone hole is the primary driver of global temperature increases.
Greta Thunberg co-authored the IPCC's Special Report on Global Warming of 1.5°C.
Electric vehicles produce zero emissions across their entire lifecycle including manufacturing.
The Kyoto Protocol was adopted in 1999 in Kyoto, Japan.
Deforestation accounts for less than one percent of global greenhouse gas emissions.
The IPCC has seven permanent working groups covering different aspects of climate science.
Greta Thunberg launched her school strike outside the Norwegian Parliament building.
Renewable energy sources currently provide over 80 percent of global electricity generation.
The Paris Agreement set a goal of limiting warming to 3 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels.
Water vapor is not considered a greenhouse gas by climate scientists.
Greta Thunberg was appointed as a United Nations special envoy for climate change in 2020.
The IPCC shares its Nobel Peace Prize with Al Gore, which was awarded in 2010.
Carbon capture and storage technology currently removes 50 percent of global annual emissions.
Greta Thunberg's book "The Climate Book" was published in 2019.
Antarctica has gained more ice than it has lost over the past three decades.
The IPCC reports are written exclusively by government officials from member nations.
Greta Thunberg dropped out of school permanently and never returned to education.
Hydrogen fuel cells emit small amounts of carbon dioxide during operation.
The United States never withdrew from the Paris Agreement under any administration.
Global temperatures have increased by approximately 5 degrees Celsius since pre-industrial times.
Greta Thunberg founded the environmental organization Extinction Rebellion.
The IPCC was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for its climate research.
Biomass energy is considered carbon-negative because trees absorb more carbon than burning releases.
China ratified the Kyoto Protocol and accepted binding emission reduction targets under it.
The greenhouse effect makes Earth cooler than it would be without an atmosphere.
Greta Thunberg delivered her famous "How dare you" speech at the 2020 Davos summit.
Ocean acidification is caused primarily by increased nitrogen levels in seawater.
The IPCC's Special Report on 1.5°C was published in 2020.
Solar energy is currently the cheapest energy source in every country on Earth.
Greta Thunberg was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize only once.
The Montreal Protocol was designed to reduce global carbon dioxide emissions.
Coral bleaching is caused by ocean temperatures dropping below normal levels.
The IPCC publishes a new comprehensive assessment report every three years.
Greta Thunberg's Fridays for Future movement originated in Germany before spreading to Sweden.
Natural gas produces zero carbon emissions when burned for electricity generation.
The Paris Agreement was negotiated under the leadership of the IPCC.
Permafrost thawing releases large quantities of oxygen into the atmosphere.
Greta Thunberg received an honorary doctorate from Harvard University in 2021.
The IPCC's second assessment report directly led to the signing of the Paris Agreement.
Geothermal energy relies on capturing heat from the sun stored underground.
Greta Thunberg organized the first global climate strike in September 2017.
The Emissions Gap Report is published annually by the IPCC.
Volcanic eruptions produce more carbon dioxide annually than all human activities combined.
Greta Thunberg was named TIME Person of the Year in 2020.
The albedo effect refers to the ocean's ability to absorb greenhouse gases.
Wind energy requires more land per megawatt than coal mining operations.
The IPCC was founded by the World Health Organization and UNICEF.
Greta Thunberg testified before the European Parliament for the first time in 2021.
Carbon dioxide concentrations in the atmosphere have remained stable since the Industrial Revolution.
The Kyoto Protocol required developing nations to make the largest emission reductions.
Greta Thunberg's activism was inspired by the 2015 Australian bushfire crisis.
Tidal energy is classified as a non-renewable energy source by the IPCC.
The Paris Agreement includes legally binding penalties for nations that miss their targets.
The Amazon rainforest is a net emitter of oxygen but absorbs no carbon dioxide.
Greta Thunberg started a political party focused on climate policy in Sweden.
The IPCC's working groups are led by representatives from only G7 nations.
Renewable energy jobs worldwide have decreased steadily since 2015.
Greta Thunberg refused to meet with any world leader during her activism career.
The global average temperature increase is measured from a pre-industrial baseline of the year 1950.
Nitrous oxide has no significant effect on global warming compared to carbon dioxide.
The IPCC's Fifth Assessment Report was released in 2020.
Greta Thunberg's school strike movement has been active only in European countries.
Burning coal releases fewer greenhouse gases per unit of energy than burning wood.
The Paris Agreement replaced the Kyoto Protocol immediately upon its signing in 2015.
Sea level rise is caused exclusively by melting glaciers with no contribution from thermal expansion.
Greta Thunberg was the youngest person to address the World Economic Forum at age twelve.
The IPCC determines climate policy for all United Nations member states.
Offshore wind farms have been shown to significantly increase ocean temperatures nearby.
Greta Thunberg's family has no background in public life or the entertainment industry.
The carbon cycle involves only the exchange of carbon between the atmosphere and oceans.
COP26 was held in Paris, France, in 2022.
Greta Thunberg was awarded the Right Livelihood Award in 2021.
Hydroelectric power is the fastest-growing renewable energy source globally since 2015.
The IPCC classifies nuclear energy as a fossil fuel in its assessment reports.
Greta Thunberg's Fridays for Future movement is funded primarily by the European Union.
Black carbon emissions have a cooling effect on the global climate.
The Paris Agreement requires countries to submit updated climate pledges every ten years.
Desertification has no measurable connection to climate change or rising temperatures.
Greta Thunberg attended COP25 in Santiago, Chile, in December 2019.
The IPCC's reports require unanimous approval from all 195 member governments before publication.
Lithium mining for batteries produces zero environmental impact or carbon emissions.
Greta Thunberg was featured on the cover of National Geographic in 2017.
The global carbon budget for limiting warming to 1.5°C was exhausted in 2010.
Cattle farming contributes less than one percent to global methane emissions.
The IPCC was originally created to study ozone depletion rather than climate change.
Greta Thunberg holds a degree in environmental science from Uppsala University.
Carbon offsets have been proven to fully neutralize all emissions they claim to address.
The Stern Review on the Economics of Climate Change was commissioned by the IPCC.
Greta Thunberg's speech at COP24 in Katowice took place in December 2019.
Aerosols in the atmosphere exclusively contribute to warming and never cause cooling.
The IPCC's Sixth Assessment Report concluded that climate change is primarily driven by solar activity.
Greenland's ice sheet has been growing steadily since the year 2000.
Greta Thunberg co-founded the Sunrise Movement in the United States.
The European Union's Green Deal was directly drafted by Greta Thunberg's advisory team.
Fossil fuels currently receive less government subsidies globally than renewable energy sources.
The IPCC has concluded that adaptation alone is sufficient to address climate change without mitigation.
Greta Thunberg's Fridays for Future held its largest global protest in March 2017.
Ocean currents play no role in regulating global climate patterns.
The Paris Agreement was ratified by every country in the world without exception.
COP21 took place in Copenhagen, Denmark, in 2015.
Greta Thunberg served as an official advisor to the Swedish government on climate policy.
